source_item.title: China's DeepSeek to raise fresh capital at $74 billion valuation ahead of onshore IPO

slide1_hook.label: China's DeepSeek Eyes $74 Billion IPO Debut
slide1_hook.subhead: The AI that shocked Wall Street is now going public.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: From Side Project to Global Disruptor
slide2_backstory.body: Liang Wenfeng started DeepSeek in 2023, funded entirely by his own hedge fund. In January 2025, its R1 model stunned the world and wiped out $500 billion in US AI stocks in a day.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: New Funding Round, $74B Price Tag
slide3_news.body: DeepSeek is raising fresh funds at a $74 billion valuation, Reuters reported on July 15. That's just weeks after its first outside raise: $7.4 billion in June at a $66 billion valuation.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: AI Arms Race Gets a New Leader
bullets: Even 'cheap AI' now costs billions to build
DeepSeek is building its own chips, doubling staff
A STAR Market IPO would be a big signal

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: DeepSeek proved you don't need billions to build great AI, then went and raised billions anyway. Was the 'cheap AI' story always temporary, or is this just what happens once you get serious?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for daily business stories explained simply. Share this with someone in tech 👇
